purpose: Determines whether yield curve steepening signals economic recovery (benign) or stress precursor (false all-clear) by decomposing into term premium vs rate expectations components.
components: Composition benchmark: stress precursors (1994, 2013) show 60-70% term premium; benign recoveries show 60-80% falling rate expectations
5y5y forward as structural neutral rate indicator (currently 4.0-4.5%)
Forward-starting swap spread divergence as leading indicator (1y forward 5y, +15-20bp vs spot)
Policy trilemma: Fed can suppress term premium, fight inflation, or allow credit stress — not all three
current_assessment: Current steepening matches stress precursor pattern at 60-70% term premium driven. 5y5y at 4.0-4.5% implies A&E delays into equivalent or worse refinancing economics. Reduced from 8/10 to 7/10 due to inherent model sensitivity.
limitations: ACM and Kim-Wright can disagree by 40-60bp
n<5 comparable episodes limits pattern matching
Novel fiscal-dominated regime may produce patterns not in historical sample
